info.name: ITEC PAYMENT
info.description:
# ITEC Payment API — Integration Guide

The **ITEC Payment API** enables businesses and developers to accept mobile money payments (MTN MoMo, Airtel, SPENN), card payments (via PesaPal), and perform cashouts/transfers — all through a simple REST interface.

---

## Base URLs

| Version | Base URL |
|---------|----------|
| **V1** | `https://pay.itecpay.rw/api` |
| **V2** | `https://pay.itecpay.rw/api2` |

> **V2 is recommended** for new integrations. It supports reference-based payment tracking (`req_ref`), richer callbacks, and a dedicated status-check endpoint.

---

## Authentication

ITEC Payment uses **API Key authentication**. Your API key is passed directly in the JSON request body as the `key` field — there are no Authorization headers required.

```json
{
  "key": "YOUR_API_KEY_HERE",
  ...
}
```

> Keep your API key secret. Never expose it in client-side code or public repositories.

---

## Quick Start

### 1. Initiate a Payment (V2 MoMo)

```http
POST https://pay.itecpay.rw/api2/pay
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "amount": 10,
  "phone": "0798760888",
  "key": "YOUR_API_KEY",
  "req_ref": "unique-reference-uuid",
  "note": "Optional note for user",
  "message": "Optional short message"
}
```

### 2. Poll for Status

```http
POST https://pay.itecpay.rw/api2/verify
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "action": "status_check",
  "req_ref": "unique-reference-uuid",
  "key": "YOUR_API_KEY"
}
```

### 3. Receive Callback

ITEC will POST a callback to your registered endpoint once the transaction is complete:

```json
{
  "status": 200,
  "data": {
    "transaction_id": "c1e8892e-89ff-4aa1-bcda-c1c51198f3bf",
    "amount": "10",
    "status": "SUCCESS"
  }
}
```

---

## API Versioning: V1 vs V2

| Feature | V1 | V2 |
|---|---|---|
| MoMo Payments | ✅ | ✅ |
| SPENN & Airtel | ✅ | ❌ |
| Card Payments (PesaPal) | ✅ | ❌ |
| Payment Reference (`req_ref`) | ❌ | ✅ |
| Status Check Endpoint | ❌ | ✅ |
| Richer Callback Payload | ❌ | ✅ |
| Recommended for new integrations | ❌ | ✅ |

---

## Integration Flow

```
Your App                        ITEC API                     Customer Phone
   |                               |                               |
   |-- POST /api2/pay -----------> |                               |
   |   { amount, phone, key,       |                               |
   |     req_ref, note, message }  |                               |
   |                               |-- Push payment prompt ------> |
   |<-- 200 { transID } ---------- |                               |
   |                               |                               |
   |   (poll or wait for callback) |                               |
   |                               |                               |
   |-- POST /api2/verify --------> |   Customer approves/rejects   |
   |   { action, req_ref, key }    |<------------------------------ |
   |<-- { status: PENDING/SUCCESS }|                               |
   |                               |                               |
   |<-- POST your_callback_url --- |  (ITEC pushes final status)   |
   |   { transaction_id, amount,   |                               |
   |     status: SUCCESS/FAILED }  |                               |
```

---

## Callback / Webhook Setup

ITEC will send a **POST request** to your callback URL when a transaction status changes. You must:

1. Register your callback URL with ITEC (contact support or configure in your dashboard).
2. Expose a publicly accessible HTTPS endpoint on your server.
3. Respond with HTTP `200 OK` to acknowledge receipt.
4. Validate the `transaction_id` and `amount` against your records before fulfilling orders.

**V2 MoMo Callback Payload:**
```json
{
  "status": 200,
  "data": {
    "transaction_id": "c1e8892e-89ff-4aa1-bcda-c1c51198f3bf",
    "amount": "10",
    "status": "SUCCESS"
  }
}
```

**V1 Cards Callback Payload:**
```json
{
  "PCODE": "BJ909000",
  "amount": "2000",
  "transID": "TYU-678-GHJ-GHJ-678"
}
```

> Always verify the transaction on your end before delivering goods or services.

---

## Error Handling

| HTTP Status | Meaning | Action |
|---|---|---|
| `200` | Success | Process the response |
| `400` | Bad Request / Unauthorized | Check your API key and request body |
| `5xx` | Server Error | Retry with exponential backoff |

**Common error response:**
```json
{
  "status": 400,
  "data": { "message": "Unauthorized" }
}
```

**Best practices:**
- Always check the `status` field in the response body (not just HTTP status code).
- Use `req_ref` (V2) to idempotently track and retry payments.
- Implement a status-polling fallback in case callbacks are delayed.
- Log all transaction IDs for reconciliation.

---

## Reports

Retrieve transaction history using the `/api/report` endpoint. Filter by type using the `report` field:

| Report Type | `report` value |
|---|---|
| All Transactions | `"all"` |
| Payments only | `"payments"` |
| Transfers/Cashouts | `"transfers"` |
| Charges | `"charges"` |

Date range is specified as `start` and `end` in `YYYYMMDDHHmmss` format (e.g., `20250101000000`).

---

## Support

For API keys, integration support, or to register your callback URL, contact **ITEC** at [tech@itec.rw](mailto:tech@itec.rw) or visit [https://pay.itecpay.rw](https://pay.itecpay.rw).

request.description:
## Request Payment (V2 — MoMo)

Initiates a mobile money payment request to a customer's phone. ITEC sends a payment prompt to the specified phone number. On success, a `transID` is returned which can be used for reconciliation. Use `req_ref` (a unique UUID you generate) to track and verify the payment status later.

---

### Request Body Parameters

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `amount` | number | ✅ | Amount to charge (in RWF) |
| `phone` | string | ✅ | Customer's phone number (e.g. `0798760888`) |
| `key` | string | ✅ | Your ITEC API key |
| `req_ref` | string | ✅ | Unique reference UUID you generate for this transaction |
| `note` | string | ❌ | Note shown to the user in their transaction report |
| `message` | string | ❌ | Short message displayed during the payment prompt |

---

### Response Fields

**Success (200)**

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `status` | number | `200` on success |
| `data.amount` | number | Amount charged |
| `data.transID` | string | ITEC's internal transaction ID |

**Failure (400)**

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `status` | number | `400` on failure |
| `data.message` | string | Error message (e.g. `"Unauthorized"`) |

---

### Code Samples

**JavaScript (fetch)**
```javascript
const response = await fetch('https://pay.itecpay.rw/api2/pay', {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
  body: JSON.stringify({
    amount: 10,
    phone: '0798760888',
    key: 'YOUR_API_KEY',
    req_ref: '15c637e1-a6ca-4788-b99f-cfe67db2fb5a',
    note: 'Payment for order #123',
    message: 'Please approve your payment'
  })
});
const data = await response.json();
console.log(data);
// { status: 200, data: { amount: 10, transID: 'd3a98870-...' } }
```

**Python (requests)**
```python
import requests

payload = {
    "amount": 10,
    "phone": "0798760888",
    "key": "YOUR_API_KEY",
    "req_ref": "15c637e1-a6ca-4788-b99f-cfe67db2fb5a",
    "note": "Payment for order #123",
    "message": "Please approve your payment"
}

response = requests.post("https://pay.itecpay.rw/api2/pay", json=payload)
print(response.json())
# {'status': 200, 'data': {'amount': 10, 'transID': 'd3a98870-...'}}
```

**PHP (cURL)**
```php
<?php
$payload = json_encode([
    "amount"  => 10,
    "phone"   => "0798760888",
    "key"     => "YOUR_API_KEY",
    "req_ref" => "15c637e1-a6ca-4788-b99f-cfe67db2fb5a",
    "note"    => "Payment for order #123",
    "message" => "Please approve your payment"
]);

$ch = curl_init("https://pay.itecpay.rw/api2/pay");
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER, true);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_POST, true);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_POSTFIELDS, $payload);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER, ["Content-Type: application/json"]);

$response = curl_exec($ch);
curl_close($ch);
echo $response;
?>
```

---

### Notes
- Always generate a **unique `req_ref`** (UUID v4) per transaction — this is used to check payment status via `/api2/verify`.
- Store the returned `transID` for reconciliation and reporting.
- After initiating, poll `/api2/verify` or wait for the callback to confirm final status.

## Get Payment Status (V2 — MoMo)

Checks the current status of a previously initiated payment using the `req_ref` you provided when calling `/api2/pay`. Returns `PENDING`, `SUCCESS`, or `FAILED`.

Use this endpoint to poll for payment confirmation if you haven't received a callback yet, or to verify a transaction before fulfilling an order.

---

### Request Body Parameters

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `action` | string | ✅ | Must be `"status_check"` |
| `req_ref` | string | ✅ | The unique reference UUID used when initiating the payment |
| `key` | string | ✅ | Your ITEC API key |

---

### Response Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `status` | number | `200` on a valid response |
| `data.transaction_id` | string | ITEC's internal transaction ID |
| `data.amount` | string | Transaction amount |
| `data.status` | string | `PENDING`, `SUCCESS`, or `FAILED` |

---

### Code Samples

**JavaScript (fetch)**
```javascript
const response = await fetch('https://pay.itecpay.rw/api2/verify', {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
  body: JSON.stringify({
    action: 'status_check',
    req_ref: 'b20adbc8-3351-4b73-947e-3f18890a7cec',
    key: 'YOUR_API_KEY'
  })
});
const data = await response.json();
console.log(data.data.status); // "PENDING" | "SUCCESS" | "FAILED"
```

**Python (requests)**
```python
import requests

payload = {
    "action": "status_check",
    "req_ref": "b20adbc8-3351-4b73-947e-3f18890a7cec",
    "key": "YOUR_API_KEY"
}

response = requests.post("https://pay.itecpay.rw/api2/verify", json=payload)
result = response.json()
print(result["data"]["status"])  # "PENDING" | "SUCCESS" | "FAILED"
```

**PHP (cURL)**
```php
<?php
$payload = json_encode([
    "action"  => "status_check",
    "req_ref" => "b20adbc8-3351-4b73-947e-3f18890a7cec",
    "key"     => "YOUR_API_KEY"
]);

$ch = curl_init("https://pay.itecpay.rw/api2/verify");
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER, true);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_POST, true);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_POSTFIELDS, $payload);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER, ["Content-Type: application/json"]);

$response = curl_exec($ch);
curl_close($ch);
$result = json_decode($response, true);
echo $result["data"]["status"]; // PENDING | SUCCESS | FAILED
?>
```

---

### Notes
- Poll this endpoint every **5–10 seconds** while status is `PENDING`.
- Stop polling once status is `SUCCESS` or `FAILED`.
- Implement a **maximum retry limit** (e.g. 12 attempts over 60 seconds) to avoid infinite loops.
- Prefer relying on the **callback** for production systems and use this endpoint as a fallback.

item.description:
This section outlines how to initiate payment requests through **MTN Mobile Money**, **Airtel Money**, and **Spenn**. These mobile payment services provide a fast, secure, and convenient way for customers to complete transactions. By integrating this functionality into your application, you can prompt users to authorize payments directly from their wallets, ensuring a seamless checkout experience.

Whether you're collecting payments for goods, services, or subscriptions, the API makes it easy to request payments, confirm their status, and manage transaction workflows efficiently. Detailed request and response structures, along with error handling, are provided to help you implement this functionality effortlessly.

item.name: MOMO-SPENN-AIRTEL

request.description:
## Request Payment (V1 — MoMo / SPENN / Airtel)

Initiates a mobile money payment request via the V1 API. Supports MTN MoMo, SPENN, and Airtel Money. A payment prompt is sent to the customer's phone. On success, a `transID` is returned.

> **Note:** For new integrations, consider using **V2** (`/api2/pay`) which supports `req_ref` for idempotent tracking and a dedicated status-check endpoint.

---

### Request Body Parameters

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `amount` | number | ✅ | Amount to charge (in RWF) |
| `phone` | string | ✅ | Customer's phone number (e.g. `0798760888`) |
| `key` | string | ✅ | Your ITEC API key |

---

### Response Fields

**Success (200)**

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `status` | number | `200` on success |
| `data.amount` | number | Amount charged |
| `data.transID` | string | ITEC's internal transaction ID |

**Failure (400)**

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `status` | number | `400` on failure |
| `data.message` | string | Error message (e.g. `"Unauthorized"`) |

---

### Code Samples

**JavaScript (fetch)**
```javascript
const response = await fetch('https://pay.itecpay.rw/api/pay', {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
  body: JSON.stringify({
    amount: 10,
    phone: '0798760888',
    key: 'YOUR_API_KEY'
  })
});
const data = await response.json();
console.log(data);
// { status: 200, data: { amount: 10, transID: 'd3a98870-...' } }
```

**Python (requests)**
```python
import requests

payload = {
    "amount": 10,
    "phone": "0798760888",
    "key": "YOUR_API_KEY"
}

response = requests.post("https://pay.itecpay.rw/api/pay", json=payload)
print(response.json())
# {'status': 200, 'data': {'amount': 10, 'transID': 'd3a98870-...'}}
```

**PHP (cURL)**
```php
<?php
$payload = json_encode([
    "amount" => 10,
    "phone"  => "0798760888",
    "key"    => "YOUR_API_KEY"
]);

$ch = curl_init("https://pay.itecpay.rw/api/pay");
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER, true);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_POST, true);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_POSTFIELDS, $payload);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER, ["Content-Type: application/json"]);

$response = curl_exec($ch);
curl_close($ch);
echo $response;
?>
```

---

### Notes
- V1 does not support `req_ref` — use the returned `transID` for tracking.
- V1 supports **SPENN** and **Airtel Money** in addition to MTN MoMo.
- There is no dedicated status-check endpoint in V1 — rely on callbacks or the Reports API for confirmation.

item.description:
This section outlines how to initiate payment requests through **MTN Mobile Money**, **Airtel Money**, and **Spenn**. These mobile payment services provide a fast, secure, and convenient way for customers to complete transactions. By integrating this functionality into your application, you can prompt users to authorize payments directly from their wallets, ensuring a seamless checkout experience.

Whether you're collecting payments for goods, services, or subscriptions, the API makes it easy to request payments, confirm their status, and manage transaction workflows efficiently. Detailed request and response structures, along with error handling, are provided to help you implement this functionality effortlessly.
